observations: Opening prison paragraphs already matched the style guide's dry, tactically concrete Sylvanas register, so I left the frost / mortar / torch imagery intact.
Replaced 'The floor seemed to shift under her' with a tactile read of cold moving through the flagstones so the sentence sounds exact instead of vaguely lyrical.
Revised Jaina's stair ascent from an internalized pain explanation to an externally legible body read, which keeps Sylvanas's narration sharp and watchful.
Reworked Lor'themar's arrival so Sylvanas reads corridor control, Farstrider presence, and visible injury before emotion; that keeps the reunion in her threat-first voice.
Kept the tribunal's clipped sentence shapes and clean noun choices, because they already sounded like the same controlling narrative intelligence as chapter23.
examples: Cell scene: 'The floor seemed to shift under her' -> 'The cold under the flagstones shifted...' for firmer sensory authority.
Stair scene: 'ordering pain to stay where it belonged' -> 'every step placed as if disorder itself might spill her open' to stay physical.
Lor'themar entrance: removed narrator interpretation about him avoiding 'the wrong thing first' and kept only what Sylvanas can see.

observations: Removed the authorial abstraction 'the difference was academic'; Sylvanas now judges only whether Ashcombe's use of fear matters in effect.
Cut the explanatory physiology in Jaina's courtroom pain beat so the narration stays with visible tension, whitened hands, and locked arm.
Changed Lor'themar's bruise beat to observation rather than motive-reading; Sylvanas can see him stop on the injury, not know why he stops.
Dropped Jaina's 'That wasn't optimism' exchange because it translated the subtext instead of letting the wall-side silence carry it.
Re-read Ashcombe, Thornton, Uther, Vereesa, and Antonidas passages for impossible knowledge; the chapter otherwise stays cleanly inside Sylvanas's read of bodies, voices, and room pressure.
examples: Exhibit scene: 'Whether Ashcombe meant to or merely understood...' -> 'Whether Ashcombe meant to or merely knew...' to keep the judgment grounded.
Lor'themar scene: 'as if cataloguing damage was the only way...' -> 'stayed there long enough to count it.'
Cell aftermath: removed direct subtext explanation after Sylvanas says 'Good.'

observations: Chapter24 still opens exactly where chapter23 leaves off: custody, thin blue torch-flame, and Frostmourne bleeding cold down into the cells.
Frostmourne remains damaged but active, consistent with the active-frostmourne-aftermath thread and the plan's warning not to imply it is solved.
Jaina stays external-only throughout; no internal POV leakage was introduced during the edit, matching the plan and the Jaina continuity notes.
Vereesa's disavowal, refusal to leave, and Lor'themar's late arrival all remain consistent with the Quel'Thalas relationship and politics memory files.
The 'elven mages can feel it through the foundations' line remains aligned with world/magic continuity and sets up the outside support without stealing chapter25's reveal.
notes: No name, title, geography, or timeline contradictions introduced.
No reader-insulting re-introduction of already known Frostmourne lore was added.

observations: The chapter deliberately uses clipped one-line beats in the cells; I preserved those where they were carrying fear rather than flattening prose.
The revised 'flagstones shifted' sentence breaks a softer cadence and lands harder against Jaina's short question-answer exchange.
Removing the extra 'That wasn't optimism / I know' exchange tightened the post-verdict wall scene and stopped it from sagging into one more explanatory beat.
Lor'themar's entrance now opens with a longer corridor-control sentence before shorter exchanges, giving the scene a better arrival pulse.
I re-checked the trial section's yes/no cross-examination monotony; it remains intentionally repetitive because Ashcombe is narrowing the room like a vice.
examples: Post-verdict scene: deleted one redundant dialogue turn to keep the beat from stuttering.
Arrival scene: widened the first Lor'themar paragraph, then let the dialogue cut back down.

continuity.crossChapter: Chapter24 now clearly carries chapter23's residue: cells, bruise, cold, custody, and the sense of being only one stairwell below the blade.
observations: The opening still acknowledges immediate dungeon aftermath rather than resetting the story in a generic prison scene.
The return-below sequence preserves causal flow: verdict, removal, passing the vault corridor, then the renewed cell confinement.
Jaina's wall-tap counting continues the same Frostmourne-clock thread that the earlier cell exchange establishes; no emotional evaporation between beats.
Lor'themar's scene now includes the Farstriders waiting in the dark and the human guard limit, which strengthens spatial continuity around his access.
The outside-support beat remains provisional—witnesses, mages, corridor leverage—without pretending the verdict has already cracked.
examples: Lor'themar entrance: added corridor-control detail so his arrival feels physically connected to the guarded wing rather than dropped in.
Removed an explanatory after-line in the Jaina wall exchange so the emotional thread carries forward instead of pausing to comment on itself.

observations: Caught and removed one surviving document-voice phrase: 'academic' in narration did not belong to this chapter's idiom.
Caught one dialogue-register leftover in Lor'themar's speech: 'administrative burden' sounded like a memorandum, not a man at a cell door.
Caught one narrator-translation habit in Lor'themar's bruise beat and replaced it with visible action.
Re-checked scene openings for throat-clearing clichés; this chapter already opens on pressure, not weather or waking, so no reset was needed.
Re-checked for stale exposition around Frostmourne, Stratholme, and Northrend; the manuscript stays in active courtroom use of facts rather than repeating lore lectures.
examples: Removed explicit explanation after Sylvanas says 'Good' in the wall scene.

observations: Most dialogue was already strong: Sylvanas clipped, Jaina pared to survival, Ashcombe precise in public legal language, Antonidas formal because he is objecting before a tribunal.
Lor'themar had the one clear institutional slip: 'administrative burden' read like office prose instead of spoken bitterness.
Changed that line to 'someone else's problem,' which keeps his contempt plainspoken while preserving his dry restraint.
Confirmed the palace mage's testimony stays simple and spoken ('gets into people's heads,' 'rooms go cold') rather than sliding into pseudo-academic explanation.
Confirmed Jaina's wall dialogue remains human under pain—short, raw, practical—and did not need embellishment.
examples: Lor'themar: 'Humans improve once condemned women become someone else's administrative burden.' -> '...someone else's problem.'

additionalPass.description: Second style-editor pass applying targeted fixes to v4.md after context compaction.
fixes: POV fix: 'evidence vault they thought a box could solve' -> 'a box had not solved' — removed omniscient mind-reading of palace officials.
Attribution over-explanation: removed 'because the room required him to be polite' — the image of the clerk's flat mouth carries itself.

POV fix: 'He hesitated from knowing exactly which answer the room wanted' -> 'He hesitated, free hand finding the linen on his burns' — behavioral grounding, calls back established detail of blistered hand.
POV fix: 'while forcing themselves back under command' cut from wall-aftermath sentence — Sylvanas cannot know Jaina's internal state through stone.

Temporal connector fix: 'Hours later they came with daylight chains' -> sensory causal bridge ('The sounds changed above her before the guards came: benches, voices, more feet on stone than any morning shift should carry. Then the daylight chains.').
Temporal connector fix: 'Much later, after the kitchen smells...' -> 'When the kitchen smells...' — sensory condition replaces temporal placeholder.
Parallel structure: 'Relay reports became preparation' -> 'Relay reports were reread as preparation' — breaks 4-sentence X-became-Y run.
Dialogue register: 'Men on watch start missing answers' -> 'Men on watch stop giving straight answers' — plainer spoken English.
Rhythm fix: 'The clerks below him kept their pens moving' -> 'Below him, the clerks kept their pens moving' — shifted sentence entry breaks the four-medium run at the verdict.

Restored deleted dialogue exchange: 'That wasn't optimism.' / 'I know.' — plan-specified beat removed by previous pass. Retained the improved breath phrasing; restored the two lines after it. This exchange is the chapter's thematic hinge — Sylvanas and Jaina acknowledging the same dark thing through a wall.
